skill.name: agent-tools
skill.description: Run 150+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more. Use when running AI apps, generating images/videos, calling LLMs, web search, or automating Twitter. Triggers: inference.sh, infsh, ai model, run ai, serverless ai, ai api, flux, veo, claude api, image generation, video generation, openrouter, tavily, exa search, twitter api, grok
skill.summary: Run 150+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown files that explain how to use the inference.sh CLI platform. All 194 static analyzer findings are false positives: the 'external_commands' detections are markdown code blocks showing example shell commands (not actual code execution), 'network' findings are documentation URLs pointing to the official inference.sh platform, 'env_access' findings document how users should configure API keys, and 'filesystem' findings show installation paths in documentation. The skill contains no executable code, no embedded scripts, and no actual network or filesystem operations.

content.user_title: Execute 150+ AI models via inference.sh CLI
content.value_statement: Developers need GPU infrastructure and multiple API integrations to use AI models. This skill provides unified CLI access to 150+ cloud-hosted AI services including image generation, video creation, LLMs, and search tools.
actual_capabilities: Run 150+ AI services via unified CLI commands without local GPU setup
Access image generation models like FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Seedream
Generate videos using Veo, Seedance, Wan, and OmniHuman models
Call LLMs including Claude, Gemini, Kimi via OpenRouter integration
Perform web search using Tavily and Exa search engines
Track asynchronous task status for long-running AI operations
limitations: Requires valid inference.sh account and API credentials to function
Requires internet connection for all AI service calls
Cannot run custom models - only supports services available on inference.sh platform
Does not execute code locally - all AI processing occurs in the cloud

use_cases.title: Developers Integrating AI Features
use_cases.description: Add image generation, video creation, or LLM capabilities to applications without managing GPU infrastructure or multiple API keys. Use CLI commands to test models and integrate via the inference.sh API.

use_cases.title: Content Creators Using Generative AI
use_cases.description: Generate images, videos, audio, and 3D models using various AI models through simple CLI commands. Compare outputs from different models like FLUX, Veo, and Gemini.

use_cases.title: Automation Engineers
use_cases.description: Automate workflows using search tools (Tavily, Exa), Twitter/X posting, and batch AI processing. Chain multiple AI services together for complex automation tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the inference.sh CLI to generate an image. Run: infsh app run falai/flux-dev-lora --input '{"prompt": "a cat astronaut floating in space"}'
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick test of image generation with FLUX model

prompt_templates.title: Video Generation with Sample Input
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video using Veo. First create sample input with 'infsh app sample google/veo-3-1-fast --save input.json', edit the prompt field, then run 'infsh app run google/veo-3-1-fast --input input.json'
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating video content with proper input validation

prompt_templates.title: LLM Integration via OpenRouter
prompt_templates.prompt: Call Claude or other LLMs through OpenRouter. Run: infsh app run openrouter/claude-sonnet-45 --input '{"prompt": "Explain quantum computing"}'

prompt_templates.title: Async Task Management
prompt_templates.prompt: For long-running tasks, use async mode: 'infsh app run google/veo-3 --input input.json --no-wait', then check status with 'infsh task get <task-id>' and retrieve results when complete
prompt_templates.scenario: Handling long-running AI operations without blocking

output_examples.input: infsh app run falai/flux-dev-lora --input '{"prompt": "a cat astronaut"}'
output_examples.output: Task completed successfully. Returns JSON with image URLs pointing to generated PNG files hosted on cloud.inference.sh

output_examples.input: infsh app run tavily/search-assistant --input '{"query": "latest AI news"}'
output_examples.output: Returns structured search results including article titles, URLs, snippets, and relevance scores from web search
Returns task status object with fields: id, status (pending/running/completed/failed), created_at, completed_at, and result data

best_practices: Generate sample input files using 'infsh app sample <app> --save input.json' to understand required input schema before running
Use async mode (--no-wait flag) for long-running tasks like video generation, then poll task status separately
Store API credentials using 'infsh login' for interactive use or INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for CI/CD pipelines
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in scripts or commit credentials to version control
Avoid running the CLI in untrusted environments since it requires API key access
Do not use --no-wait for quick tasks that complete in seconds - adds unnecessary complexity

faq.question: What is inference.sh and how does it work?
faq.answer: inference.sh is a cloud platform that hosts 150+ AI models and services. The CLI tool authenticates with the platform and executes AI tasks remotely, returning results without requiring local GPU hardware.

faq.question: Which AI models are available through this skill?
faq.answer: Access image models (FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream), video models (Veo, Seedance, Wan, OmniHuman), LLMs (Claude, Gemini, Kimi via OpenRouter), search tools (Tavily, Exa), and utilities like TTS and 3D generation.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run 'infsh login' to open a browser-based authentication flow. For CI/CD, set the INFSH_API_KEY environment variable with your API key from the inference.sh dashboard.

faq.question: Can I run AI models locally with this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill connects to cloud-hosted AI services on the inference.sh platform. All model execution happens remotely on their infrastructure, not on your local machine.

faq.question: How are long-running tasks handled?
faq.answer: Use the --no-wait flag to submit tasks asynchronously. The CLI returns a task ID immediately. Check status anytime with 'infsh task get <task-id>' and retrieve results when complete.

faq.question: Can I use custom AI models with inference.sh?
faq.answer: This skill only supports models available on the inference.sh platform. You cannot upload or run custom models. Browse available models with 'infsh app list' or search with 'infsh app search'.
